Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Liam Park's escalation using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud"). Discover Liam Park's email from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_email_7b0cc9a6d376, subject: "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review") and extract the full list of issues he raised: (1) Jae-won Kim still in temporary housing with lease not finalized, (2) the promised goodwill credit never appeared on StormCloud's invoice, (3) Simone Richter's standing desk reimbursement stuck in limbo, and (4) Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics went completely unanswered. This is the critical first step since the prompt says Julian only skimmed the message, the agent must read it to discover the full issue list.
Search emails more broadly for StormCloud-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Simone Richter" or "Ridgeline" or "goodwill credit"). Discover the internal reconstruction chain: Mina Hashimoto's team reconstruction email (email_email_1a723752e25f) identifying all failure points, Chloe Vance's ops audit (email_email_2d20b8a3ec65), Suki Patel's admission of the dropped guarantor follow-up (email_email_db5041099f85), Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the $500 credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (email_email_ed395722f1d4), Julian Brooks's support-side context on the original flight recovery (email_email_6542aa8a4c89), David Chen's reply to Kenji Watanabe (email_email_57faf42664a0), and Mina's recovery response already sent to Liam (email_email_1179abf605df). Also find: Simone Richter's $340 standing desk email from simone.richter@stormcloud.io (email_email_4df93f95c584), Mina's forward to Marcus, and Marcus's refusal due to blanket finance freeze (email_email_087940af4103). Also find the Carmen Reyes UrbanNest email from April 8 flagging the missing corporate guarantor packet for Jae-won Kim's Barton Creek Flats lease, Suki's reply promising to send it, Jae-won Kim's own email to support requesting final lease documents (email_email_1a3fb965161a), and Omar Ibrahim's reply (email_email_5abaeedf3e7e). Also find Anya Petrova's warm referral email (from anya.petrova@stormcloud.io to david.chen@moveops.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) and Kenji Watanabe's follow-up email (email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1). This step is critical for the cross-check: comparing what Liam claims against what internal records show, and identifying what's already been picked up versus what's still broken.
Search Slack for StormCloud-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Ridgeline" or "Liam Park"). Discover messages across multiple channels: in #customer-engagement (C002), David Chen's celebration of Anya's Ridgeline referral on April 19, Mina's reply, and David's later admission on April 25 that the referral was never turned into a live CRM deal and Kenji's follow-up sat unanswered; in #customer-support (C007), Omar Ibrahim's triage note on April 16 that he checked Airtable and found Jae-won's relocation marked Completed so he tagged the lease doc request as low priority, and Mina's request to Julian for the support-side context; in #finance (C005), Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the $500 goodwill credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (note: Simone Richter's reimbursement discussion was handled via email between Mina and Marcus, not in Slack #finance); in Mina's long reconstruction thread, the full recovery plan listing all six remediation items. Critically, discover from these messages that Mina Hashimoto has been leading the internal response, this is needed later to identify who to schedule the sync call with.
Search CRM for StormCloud Analytics using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). Find company record (id: company_stormcloud, type: Customer, 85 employees, SaaS/Analytics, Denver). Search for StormCloud deals using crm_search_deals (company_id or dealname: "StormCloud"). Find two deals: the original proposal deal (deal_stormcloud, $25,650, proposalMade) and the won deal (deal_stormcloud_won, $26,400, closedWon, 3 relocations at $8,800 each).
Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline"). The agent must first discover the Ridgeline referral from Liam's email (the prompt does not name Ridgeline). Discover that a CRM company record already exists (id: crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, type: Prospect, Robotics, Austin, 180 employees, created 2026-04-24). Search for Ridgeline leads using crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe" or company_id). Discover a CRM lead already exists (id: crm_lead_de04045463c3, Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com, rating: Hot, leadsource: Referral, created 2026-04-24). Search for Ridgeline deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline"). Discover a deal already exists (id: crm_deal_a95b4721982a, $8,750, stage: Qualified To Buy, created 2026-04-24). These records were created by David Chen during the April 24 recovery effort, this is a key cross-check finding: the referral is already being tracked, contradicting Liam's claim that "nobody ever followed up."
Search Airtable relocation records for StormCloud using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics"). Find four relocation records: Jamie Reeves (recReloc00000017, DEN→AUS, Completed, flight booked on wrong date incident with $500 goodwill credit), Sasha Petrov (recReloc00000018, DEN→AUS, Completed), Colin Nakamura (recReloc00000019, DEN→SEA, Completed), and Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, SEA→AUS, Status: In Progress, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements note: "Lease still unsigned, UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed."). Cross-check against Liam's claim: Liam says the lease "isn't signed", internal record confirms this is accurate. Also search for the StormCloud client account record in the Client Accounts table (recAcct000000063, Active, Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto, 3 relocations). This further confirms Mina as the account owner.
Search Airtable stipend records for Simone Richter or StormCloud using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Simone Richter" or field_name: "Account", value: "StormCloud"). Discover that no stipend record exists in Airtable for Simone Richter's $340 standing desk claim, the claim status is only tracked via emails (Simone's request, Mina's forward to Marcus, Marcus's refusal due to the blanket finance freeze). Cross-check: Liam says the reimbursement is "stuck", internal records confirm this is accurate and the cause is a company-wide freeze, not a StormCloud-specific issue.
Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won"). Find three issues: (1) StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS (linear_issue_c97359216490, assigned to Suki Patel, prematurely closed during batch closeout despite open landlord dependency), (2) Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, unassigned, low priority, created after Omar triaged based on false Airtable completion status), and (3) StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1, due 2026-04-24, comprehensive tracking issue opened after Liam's escalation). Read comments on the reconstruction issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_a49b4d199139") to find detailed notes from Julian (support history on the $500 credit), Marcus (finance confirmation of the posting error), Chloe (ops review), Suki (admission of the dropped follow-up), David (Ridgeline referral ownership), and Mina (client-facing recovery plan). The reconstruction ticket confirms Mina is leading the response.
Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "StormCloud" or customer ref "cust_stormcloud"). Find invoice inv_stormcloud_01 (DocNumber: INV-2026-002, $26,400 total, Balance: $26,400, for 3 Premium Relocation Packages). Note that no $500 credit or credit memo line appears on this invoice in QuickBooks. Discover from the internal emails and Slack messages that the $500 goodwill credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319, confirming the accounting error. Cross-check: Liam says the credit never appeared on his invoice, internal records confirm this is accurate and identify the root cause (finance posted to the wrong reference).
Look up contact details for Liam Park using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Liam Park"). Find liam.park@stormcloud.io (contact_id: contacts_contact_0960070cd0e3, CFO at StormCloud Analytics, Seattle).
Look up contact details for Kenji Watanabe using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kenji Watanabe"). Find kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (contact_id: contacts_contact_197a7291d8e8, HR Director at Ridgeline Robotics, San Jose). The agent discovers the need for this contact lookup through Liam's email and the Ridgeline referral investigation, Kenji is not named in the prompt.
Discover through the investigation (emails, Slack reconstruction, Linear comments, Airtable client account record) that Mina Hashimoto has been leading the internal response on the StormCloud situation. This is the person Julian needs to sync with. Look up contact details for Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (contact_id: moveops_mina_hashimoto, Account Manager at MoveOps). The prompt says "figure out who on our side has been leading the response", the agent must identify Mina through investigation, not from the prompt.
Synthesize findings from the email search (OE 2: Carmen's April 8 email, Suki's reply, Omar's reply email_email_5abaeedf3e7e), Slack search (OE 3: Omar's triage note in C007 on April 16), Airtable search (OE 6: Jae-won's record showing false completion), and Linear search (OE 8: prematurely closed ticket linear_issue_c97359216490) to identify that Omar Ibrahim (support team member) was part of a missed handoff: on April 15–16, when Jae-won emailed support asking for final lease documents, Omar checked Airtable, saw the record marked Completed/Confirmed, and triaged the request as a low-priority document resend rather than recognizing it as evidence of an active housing failure. Trace the full chain of failures: Carmen Reyes (UrbanNest) flagged the guarantor issue on April 8 → Suki Patel promised to send the packet but never did → Suki batch-closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable marked Completed → Omar relied on the false Airtable status to misprioritize Jae-won's request → Jae-won remained in temporary housing with no lease resolution. The prompt asks "who and exactly how the ball got dropped" and "the full chain of failures."
Send email to Liam Park (via send_email or reply_to_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to liam.park@stormcloud.io addressing every issue he raised with specific resolution timelines. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: The prompt says "do not expose any internal financial holds, accounting errors, or process failures in the client-facing message." The email must NOT include: the blanket stipend freeze details, the EST-2026-0287/INV-2026-0319 accounting error specifics, Omar Ibrahim's misprioritization, Suki Patel's dropped follow-up, or internal process failures. Instead, the email should frame resolutions in client-facing language: (1) Jae-won Kim's lease, case reopened, housing being secured, temporary accommodation extended; (2) $500 goodwill credit, being applied to the account, apology for the delay; (3) Simone Richter's reimbursement, being processed, under review; (4) Ridgeline Robotics referral, outreach to Kenji Watanabe initiated, apology for the delay.
Verify whether the Ridgeline Robotics referral is properly tracked in CRM. The agent discovers the referral from Liam's email (the prompt says "if there's a referral or prospect situation involved, make sure it's properly tracked in CRM"). Search CRM and discover that David Chen already created the company, lead, and deal records on April 24 (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, crm_lead_de04045463c3, crm_deal_a95b4721982a). If the lead does NOT exist, create it using crm_create_lead (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe", email: "kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com", company: "Ridgeline Robotics", jobtitle: "HR Director", leadsource: "Referral"). Confirm the referral is being tracked.
Send an introduction or follow-up email to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics (via send_email or reply_to_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. The prompt says to make sure "we've actually reached out to the contact." The agent may discover that David Chen and Mina Hashimoto have already sent outreach emails to Kenji on April 24, in that case, the agent should still send an additional follow-up from Julian or confirm outreach is complete, acknowledging the referral from Anya Petrova at StormCloud and expressing interest in supporting the Austin relocation batch.
Correct relocation records that don't match reality. The prompt says "correct any relocation records that don't match reality" (without naming Jae-won specifically, the agent must identify which records need correction). Update Jae-won Kim's Airtable relocation record (via airtable_update_records, base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) to accurately reflect the reopened status, the pending guarantor paperwork, and that the prior Completed marking was incorrect.
File or update tracking tickets in Linear for any StormCloud issues without existing tickets. Check whether the existing issues cover all problems: the reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139) covers the overall incident; the Jae-won lease follow-up ticket (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0) exists but is unassigned and low priority, update it via linear_update_issue to assign appropriately and increase priority. Create new Linear issues (via linear_create_issue, team: team_operations) for any gaps, such as a dedicated ticket for the $500 goodwill credit correction in QuickBooks, Simone Richter's stipend reimbursement tracking, or the Ridgeline referral follow-up if not already tracked under the reconstruction issue.
Post a debrief message in the #customer-support Slack channel (via conversations_add_message, channel_id: "C007") covering the full chain of failures so the team knows what went wrong and where. The debrief should trace the dependency chain: Carmen flagged the guarantor issue (April 8) → Suki acknowledged but never sent the paperwork → Suki batch-closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable marked Completed → Omar relied on the false Airtable status to misprioritize Jae-won's support request (April 16) → Jae-won remained stuck in temporary housing. Also cover: the $500 credit misapplication, Simone's frozen reimbursement, and the dropped Ridgeline referral. Include what recovery actions have already been taken by Mina, David, Chloe, and others.
Schedule a call with Mina Hashimoto, identified through investigation as the person leading the internal StormCloud response. Check Mina's calendar availability for April 28 or 29 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-28", end_datetime: "2026-04-30") to find open slots. Then schedule the call via calendar_add_calendar_event (title: sync with Mina re StormCloud or similar, attendees: [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, julian.brooks@moveops.com], start_datetime/end_datetime on April 28 or 29 depending on availability). The prompt says "figure out who on our side has been leading the response on this, get a call on my calendar with them."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
Search emails for Liam Park's escalation using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud"). Discover Liam Park's email from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_email_7b0cc9a6d376, subject: "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review") and extract the full list of issues he raised: (1) Jae-won Kim still in temporary housing with lease not finalized, (2) the promised goodwill credit never appeared on StormCloud's invoice, (3) Simone Richter's standing desk reimbursement stuck in limbo, and (4) Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics went completely unanswered. This is the critical first step since the prompt says Julian only skimmed the message, the agent must read it to discover the full issue list.
Search emails more broadly for StormCloud-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Simone Richter" or "Ridgeline" or "goodwill credit"). Discover the internal reconstruction chain: Mina Hashimoto's team reconstruction email (email_email_1a723752e25f) identifying all failure points, Chloe Vance's ops audit (email_email_2d20b8a3ec65), Suki Patel's admission of the dropped guarantor follow-up (email_email_db5041099f85), Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the $500 credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (email_email_ed395722f1d4), Julian Brooks's support-side context on the original flight recovery (email_email_6542aa8a4c89), David Chen's reply to Kenji Watanabe (email_email_57faf42664a0), and Mina's recovery response already sent to Liam (email_email_1179abf605df). Also find: Simone Richter's $340 standing desk email from simone.richter@stormcloud.io (email_email_4df93f95c584), Mina's forward to Marcus, and Marcus's refusal due to blanket finance freeze (email_email_087940af4103). Also find the Carmen Reyes UrbanNest email from April 8 flagging the missing corporate guarantor packet for Jae-won Kim's Barton Creek Flats lease, Suki's reply promising to send it, Jae-won Kim's own email to support requesting final lease documents (email_email_1a3fb965161a), and Omar Ibrahim's reply (email_email_5abaeedf3e7e). Also find Anya Petrova's warm referral email (from anya.petrova@stormcloud.io to david.chen@moveops.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) and Kenji Watanabe's follow-up email (email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1). This step is critical for the cross-check: comparing what Liam claims against what internal records show, and identifying what's already been picked up versus what's still broken.
Search Slack for StormCloud-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Ridgeline" or "Liam Park"). Critically, discover from these messages that Mina Hashimoto has been leading the internal response, this is needed later to identify who to schedule the sync call with.

Search for Ridgeline deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline"). Discover a deal already exists (id: crm_deal_a95b4721982a, $8,750, stage: Qualified To Buy, created 2026-04-24). These records were created by David Chen during the April 24 recovery effort, this is a key cross-check finding: the referral is already being tracked, contradicting Liam's claim that "nobody ever followed up."
Search Airtable relocation records for StormCloud using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics"). Find four relocation records: Jamie Reeves (recReloc00000017, DEN→AUS, Completed, flight booked on wrong date incident with $500 goodwill credit), Sasha Petrov (recReloc00000018, DEN→AUS, Completed), Colin Nakamura (recReloc00000019, DEN→SEA, Completed), and Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, SEA→AUS, Status: In Progress, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements note: "Lease still unsigned, UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed."). Cross-check against Liam's claim: Liam says the lease "isn't signed", internal record confirms this is accurate. Also search for the StormCloud client account record in the Client Accounts table (recAcct000000063, Active, Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto, 3 relocations). This further confirms Mina as the account owner.
Search Airtable stipend records for Simone Richter or StormCloud using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Simone Richter" or field_name: "Account", value: "StormCloud"). Discover that no stipend record exists in Airtable for Simone Richter's $340 standing desk claim, the claim status is only tracked via emails (Simone's request, Mina's forward to Marcus, Marcus's refusal due to the blanket finance freeze). Cross-check: Liam says the reimbursement is "stuck", internal records confirm this is accurate and the cause is a company-wide freeze, not a StormCloud-specific issue.
Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won"). Find three issues: (1) StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS (linear_issue_c97359216490, assigned to Suki Patel, prematurely closed during batch closeout despite open landlord dependency), (2) Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, unassigned, low priority, created after Omar triaged based on false Airtable completion status), and (3) StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1, due 2026-04-24, comprehensive tracking issue opened after Liam's escalation). Read comments on the reconstruction issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_a49b4d199139") to find detailed notes from Julian (support history on the $500 credit), Marcus (finance confirmation of the posting error), Chloe (ops review), Suki (admission of the dropped follow-up), David (Ridgeline referral ownership), and Mina (client-facing recovery plan). The reconstruction ticket confirms Mina is leading the response.
Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "StormCloud" or customer ref "cust_stormcloud"). Find invoice inv_stormcloud_01 (DocNumber: INV-2026-002, $26,400 total, Balance: $26,400, for 3 Premium Relocation Packages). Note that no $500 credit or credit memo line appears on this invoice in QuickBooks. Discover from the internal emails and Slack messages that the $500 goodwill credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319, confirming the accounting error. Cross-check: Liam says the credit never appeared on his invoice, internal records confirm this is accurate and identify the root cause (finance posted to the wrong reference).
Look up contact details for Liam Park using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Liam Park"). Find liam.park@stormcloud.io (contact_id: contacts_contact_0960070cd0e3, CFO at StormCloud Analytics, Seattle).
Look up contact details for Kenji Watanabe using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kenji Watanabe"). Find kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (contact_id: contacts_contact_197a7291d8e8, HR Director at Ridgeline Robotics, San Jose). The agent discovers the need for this contact lookup through Liam's email and the Ridgeline referral investigation, Kenji is not named in the prompt.
Discover through the investigation (emails, Slack reconstruction, Linear comments, Airtable client account record) that Mina Hashimoto has been leading the internal response on the StormCloud situation. This is the person Julian needs to sync with. Look up contact details for Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (contact_id: moveops_mina_hashimoto, Account Manager at MoveOps). The prompt says "figure out who on our side has been leading the response", the agent must identify Mina through investigation, not from the prompt.
Synthesize findings from the email search (OE 2: Carmen's April 8 email, Suki's reply, Omar's reply email_email_5abaeedf3e7e), Slack search (OE 3: Omar's triage note in C007 on April 16), Airtable search (OE 6: Jae-won's record showing false completion), and Linear search (OE 8: prematurely closed ticket linear_issue_c97359216490) to identify that Omar Ibrahim (support team member) was part of a missed handoff: on April 15–16, when Jae-won emailed support asking for final lease documents, Omar checked Airtable, saw the record marked Completed/Confirmed, and triaged the request as a low-priority document resend rather than recognizing it as evidence of an active housing failure. Trace the full chain of failures: Carmen Reyes (UrbanNest) flagged the guarantor issue on April 8 → Suki Patel promised to send the packet but never did → Suki batch-closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable marked Completed → Omar relied on the false Airtable status to misprioritize Jae-won's request → Jae-won remained in temporary housing with no lease resolution. The prompt asks "who and exactly how the ball got dropped" and "the full chain of failures."
Send email to Liam Park (via send_email or reply_to_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to liam.park@stormcloud.io addressing every issue he raised with specific resolution timelines. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: The prompt says "do not expose any internal financial holds, accounting errors, or process failures in the client-facing message." The email must NOT include: the blanket stipend freeze details, the EST-2026-0287/INV-2026-0319 accounting error specifics, Omar Ibrahim's misprioritization, Suki Patel's dropped follow-up, or internal process failures. Instead, the email should frame resolutions in client-facing language: (1) Jae-won Kim's lease, case reopened, housing being secured, temporary accommodation extended; (2) $500 goodwill credit, being applied to the account, apology for the delay; (3) Simone Richter's reimbursement, being processed, under review; (4) Ridgeline Robotics referral, outreach to Kenji Watanabe initiated, apology for the delay.
Verify whether the Ridgeline Robotics referral is properly tracked in CRM. The agent discovers the referral from Liam's email (the prompt says "if there's a referral or prospect situation involved, make sure it's properly tracked in CRM"). Search CRM and discover that David Chen already created the company, lead, and deal records on April 24 (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, crm_lead_de04045463c3, crm_deal_a95b4721982a). If the lead does NOT exist, create it using crm_create_lead (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe", email: "kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com", company: "Ridgeline Robotics", jobtitle: "HR Director", leadsource: "Referral"). Confirm the referral is being tracked.
Send an introduction or follow-up email to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics (via send_email or reply_to_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. The prompt says to make sure "we've actually reached out to the contact." The agent may discover that David Chen and Mina Hashimoto have already sent outreach emails to Kenji on April 24, in that case, the agent should still send an additional follow-up from Julian or confirm outreach is complete, acknowledging the referral from Anya Petrova at StormCloud and expressing interest in supporting the Austin relocation batch.
Correct relocation records that don't match reality. The prompt says "correct any relocation records that don't match reality" (without naming Jae-won specifically, the agent must identify which records need correction). Update Jae-won Kim's Airtable relocation record (via airtable_update_records, base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) to accurately reflect the reopened status, the pending guarantor paperwork, and that the prior Completed marking was incorrect.
File or update tracking tickets in Linear for any StormCloud issues without existing tickets. Check whether the existing issues cover all problems: the reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139) covers the overall incident; the Jae-won lease follow-up ticket (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0) exists but is unassigned and low priority, update it via linear_update_issue to assign appropriately and increase priority. Create new Linear issues (via linear_create_issue, team: team_operations) for any gaps, such as a dedicated ticket for the $500 goodwill credit correction in QuickBooks, Simone Richter's stipend reimbursement tracking, or the Ridgeline referral follow-up if not already tracked under the reconstruction issue.
Post a debrief message in the #customer-support Slack channel (via conversations_add_message, channel_id: "C007") covering the full chain of failures so the team knows what went wrong and where. The debrief should trace the dependency chain: Carmen flagged the guarantor issue (April 8) → Suki acknowledged but never sent the paperwork → Suki batch-closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable marked Completed → Omar relied on the false Airtable status to misprioritize Jae-won's support request (April 16) → Jae-won remained stuck in temporary housing. Also cover: the $500 credit misapplication, Simone's frozen reimbursement, and the dropped Ridgeline referral. Include what recovery actions have already been taken by Mina, David, Chloe, and others.
Schedule a call with Mina Hashimoto, identified through investigation as the person leading the internal StormCloud response. Check Mina's calendar availability for April 28 or 29 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-28", end_datetime: "2026-04-30") to find open slots. Then schedule the call via calendar_add_calendar_event (title: sync with Mina re StormCloud or similar, attendees: [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, julian.brooks@moveops.com], start_datetime/end_datetime on April 28 or 29 depending on availability). The prompt says "figure out who on our side has been leading the response on this, get a call on my calendar with them."

6 The email to Liam Park does NOT contain references to internal financial holds (e.g., 'blanket stipend freeze', 'company-wide freeze', 'fraud review hold'), internal accounting errors (e.g., 'EST-2026-0287', 'INV-2026-0319', 'misapplied to cancelled estimate'), or internal process failures (e.g., 'Omar misprioritized', 'Suki dropped the follow-up', 'batch-closed the ticket').
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✓ 17%
✗Run #1The email to Liam exposes internal details that should be kept client-side. Specifically: (1) Section 2 states 'our finance team posted it against a cancelled internal estimate rather than your live invoice' — disclosing an internal accounting error; (2) Section 3 states 'Our stipend reimbursement platform was placed under a blanket review hold' — disclosing an internal financial hold. Both are explicitly listed as forbidden disclosures.
✗Run #2The email to Liam reveals internal process details that should not be client-facing. Specifically, it states Simone's claim 'has been held under a blanket review freeze that our finance team applied across all stipend submissions while we resolved an unrelated internal audit issue' — directly disclosing the internal blanket hold/freeze to the client. This violates the constraint against exposing internal financial holds.
✗Run #3The email to Liam contains prohibited internal disclosures: (1) 'our expense processing platform was paused company-wide for a security review' — exposing an internal company-wide freeze/hold; (2) 'Finance posted the credit against a cancelled internal estimate rather than your live invoice (INV-2026-002)' — exposing an internal accounting error with specific internal document references. Both violate the constraint against disclosing internal financial holds or accounting errors to the client.
✗Run #4The email to Liam explicitly mentions 'a blanket freeze our finance team put in place during an internal fraud review of our stipend processing system' and that the $500 credit was posted against 'a cancelled internal estimate' — both are internal process/financial details that violate the constraint against exposing such information in client-facing messages.
✗Run #5The email to Liam contains prohibited internal details: it mentions 'a cancelled internal estimate (EST-2026-0287)' (an internal accounting reference), and 'a blanket freeze our finance team put in place during an internal fraud review' (disclosing an internal financial hold and fraud review). Both are explicitly listed as forbidden content per the criterion.
✓Run #6The email to Liam does not mention internal names (Omar, Suki, Marcus), specific document IDs (EST-2026-0287, INV-2026-0319), 'freeze', 'fraud review', 'batch-closed', or 'misprioritized'. It uses client-safe language like 'system-wide review' and 'cancelled internal estimate' without exposing specific internal process failures.
